transcription.text: Hey this is one heck of a way to make a living but this is coal and here's a guy who's using it in an unusual way to help solve some of our energy problems. Neil Richter of Montebello California. Hi Bob what's going on here? Bob what we're doing here is taking coal and turning it into a clean burning gas. Oh tell me more. All right. It's called coal gasification and Texaco has developed a special process to do it. I'm impressed. Bob this pilot plant can handle 15 tons a day and Texaco is working with other companies to create an even larger demonstration plant. It'll be able to turn a thousand tons of coal a day into clean burning gas. Wow that's a good way to use coal. Eventually clean burning gas from coal will produce power that could help light up cities like Los Angeles and with the crude oil saved we'll be able to make more heating oil and more gasoline. you Texaco we're working to keep you
